title: A chain of governance failures, not a single scandal
subtitle: Five named controversies the opposition framed as 'incompetence and irresponsibility'
thesis: The 22nd election framed Yoon's tenure as a sequence of avoidable governance failures, not a single defining scandal. Itaewon disaster response (Oct 2022), the Chae Sang-byeol investigation-pressure case (July 2023), the 2023 World Scout Jamboree mismanagement, the failed Busan Expo 2030 bid, and Kim Keon-hee first-lady controversies each contributed to a cumulative narrative — and the chain was harder to defend against than any single incident would have been.

sections.heading: The Itaewon disaster as inflection point
paragraphs: On October 29, 2022, a Halloween crowd crush in a 45-meter alley in Itaewon, Yongsan-gu killed 159 people (133 Korean, 26 foreign nationals) and injured 196 more. The investigation revealed that police had received 11+ emergency calls about crowd danger over the four hours before the incident, and 79+ calls in total before it happened. Only 137 officers had been deployed to Itaewon, while a 25,000-person protest elsewhere in Seoul that evening had 6,500 officers.

A November 2022 poll found 7 of 10 Koreans believed the government was responsible. 57% considered the official investigation insufficient. 23 officers were eventually charged with manslaughter and negligence; Interior Minister Lee Sang-min was suspended by the National Assembly in February 2023. The disaster was not just a tragedy but a governance pattern: warnings ignored, accountability deflected.

sections.heading: The Chae Sang-byeol case (채상병 사건)
paragraphs: On July 19, 2023, Marine Corporal Chae Su-geun (referred to in Korean political discourse as 채상병 / Corporal Chae) died during a flood rescue operation in Yecheon County after being reassigned from ground searches to underwater work without a life jacket. The Marine Corps investigation initially identified eight responsible parties; the Defense Ministry then halted the handover to police and revised the report to name only two. Defense Minister Lee Jong-sup was charged investigation-head with insubordination — a reversal that became the political story.

The pattern repeated: Lee Jong-sup was appointed ambassador to Australia in March 2024 — three weeks before the election — and resigned the post within weeks under continued pressure. The case crystallized a perception that the administration was prioritizing institutional protection over accountability. wiki-22-ko names this incident specifically as one of the named drivers of the 22nd election outcome.

sections.heading: Three more incidents in the chain
paragraphs: Korean Wikipedia's election summary names two further controversies: the 2023 World Scout Jamboree (잼버리 파행), where ~43,000 international scouts were evacuated from a poorly-prepared Saemangeum site as a typhoon approached, becoming a global embarrassment; and the November 2023 failed Busan World Expo 2030 bid, which Yoon had personally championed, losing decisively to Riyadh in the BIE vote. Both were preventable failures of state coordination.

Adding to the chain, first-lady Kim Keon-hee faced sustained controversy over a 2022 hidden-camera video showing her accepting a Christian Dior handbag, and stock-manipulation and academic-credential allegations. The Cho Kuk Innovation Party explicitly demanded a special prosecutor investigation; PPP defenders argued the issues were politically motivated. Either reading, the controversy did not subside through the 2024 campaign.

sections.heading: Why the chain mattered more than any single incident
paragraphs: A single scandal can be defended, deflected, or absorbed. A *chain* of named controversies with similar pattern — warnings ignored, accountability deflected, institutional protection prioritized — produces a narrative that's hard to counter, because each new incident reinforces the prior reading. By March 2024, the opposition could simply name the chain ('Itaewon, Chae, Jamboree, Expo, Kim Keon-hee') and the rhetorical work was largely done.

The Korean phrase that emerged was '무능력과 무책임' — incompetence and irresponsibility. wiki-22-ko frames this as the opposition's most effective campaign-period synthesis. Whether each individual claim of culpability is fair is a separate question; for *electoral* purposes, the chain functioned as a single structural argument that voters carried into the booth.

key_data_points.label: Chae Su-geun (Corporal Chae) death
key_data_points.value: Marine death during flood rescue; investigation-pressure controversy followed

key_data_points.label: Defense Minister Lee Jong-sup
key_data_points.value: Halted Chae investigation handover; appointed Australia ambassador March 2024; resigned within weeks under pressure

key_data_points.label: Named controversy chain (per wiki-22-ko)
key_data_points.value: Itaewon · Chae · 2023 Jamboree · Busan Expo · Kim Keon-hee
